title: Ensure Oslogin Is Enabled for a Project

input_row.title: Ensure Oslogin Is Enabled for a Project
input_row.description: Enabling OS login binds SSH certificates to IAM users and facilitates effective SSH certificate management.
input_row.rationale: Enabling osLogin ensures that SSH keys used to connect to instances are mapped with IAM users. Revoking access to IAM user will revoke all the SSH keys associated with that particular user. It facilitates centralized and automated SSH key pair management which is useful in handling cases like response to compromised SSH key pairs and/or revocation of external/third-party/Vendor users.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the VM compute metadata page by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/compute/metadata. 2. Ensure that key enable-oslogin is present with value set to TRUE. 3. Because instances can override project settings, ensure that no instance has custom metadata with key enable-oslogin and value FALSE. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List the instances in your project and get details on each instance: gcloud compute instances list --format=json 2. Verify that the section commonInstanceMetadata has a key enable-oslogin set to value TRUE. Exception: VMs created by GKE should be excluded. These VMs have names that start with gke- and are labeled goog-gke-node
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the VM compute metadata page by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/compute/metadata. 2. Click Edit. 3. Add a metadata entry where the key is enable-oslogin and the value is TRUE. 4. Click Save to apply the changes. 5. For every instance that overrides the project setting, go to the VM Instances page at https://console.cloud.google.com/compute/instances. 6. Click the name of the instance on which you want to remove the metadata value. 7. At the top of the instance details page, click Edit to edit the instance settings. 8. Under Custom metadata, remove any entry with key enable-oslogin and the value is FALSE 9. At the bottom of the instance details page, click Save to apply your changes to the instance. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Configure oslogin on the project: gcloud compute project-info add-metadata --metadata enable-oslogin=TRUE 2. Remove instance metadata that overrides the project setting. gcloud compute instances remove-metadata --keys=enable- oslogin Optionally, you can enable two factor authentication for OS login. For more information, see: https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/oslogin/setup-two-factor-authentication.
input_row.impact: Enabling OS Login on project disables metadata-based SSH key configurations on all instances from a project. Disabling OS Login restores SSH keys that you have configured in project or instance meta-data.
input_row.default_value: By default, parameter enable-oslogin is not set, which is equivalent to setting it to FALSE.
input_row.additional_information: 1. In order to use osLogin, instance using Custom Images must have the latest version of the Linux Guest Environment installed. The following image families do not yet support OS Login: Project cos-cloud (Container-Optimized OS) image family cos-stable. All project coreos-cloud (CoreOS) image families Project suse-cloud (SLES) image family sles-11 All Windows Server and SQL Server image families 2. Project enable-oslogin can be over-ridden by setting enable-oslogin parameter to an instance metadata individually.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit can be fully verified via GCP APIs and GCP CLI.
- The audit mentions Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands to list instances and verify the status of the "enable-oslogin" metadata setting.
- It involves standard GCP service properties related to virtual machine instance access, which are exposed through APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_compute_instance_os_login_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **GCP API Calls:** Use the following GCP API methods:
   - `GET https://compute.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/{project}/globalMetadata` to retrieve project-wide metadata settings.
   - `GET https://compute.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/{project}/zones/{zone}/instanceGroupManagers/{instanceGroupManager}` to check specific instance metadata.

2. **Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) Commands:** The following command will be used:
   - `gcloud compute project-info describe --format=json` to check the project metadata for the "enable-oslogin" entry.
   - `gcloud compute instances list --format=json` to list instances and check their common instance metadata settings.

3. **Resources to Enumerate:** 
   - All VM instances within the specified GCP project will be checked.
   - Verify instance metadata for VM instances, ensuring they don’t have custom metadata entries that override the project setting.

4. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - The project-level metadata key "enable-oslogin" should be set to TRUE.
   - Each instance should NOT have custom metadata key "enable-oslogin" set to FALSE.

5. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - A PASS is when the project-level setting for "enable-oslogin" is TRUE and none of the instances have the metadata "enable-oslogin" with the value FALSE.
   - A FAIL is reported if either the project-level value is FALSE, or any instance overrides the setting by having "enable-oslogin" explicitly set to FALSE.

This comprehensive automated approach aligns with the CIS compliance control stipulated for ensuring OS login is enabled for a GCP project, facilitating effective SSH key management and compliance within GCP environments.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification as Automated is correct. The audit can indeed be fully verified using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. The necessary metadata settings for enabling OS Login are accessible via these tools, making manual verification unnecessary.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are largely accurate. The `gcloud compute project-info describe --format=json` command is valid for retrieving project-level metadata, including "enable-oslogin". Similarly, `gcloud compute instances list --format=json` is appropriate for listing instances and checking their metadata.
- However, the API method `GET https://compute.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/{project}/zones/{zone}/instanceGroupManagers/{instanceGroupManager}` is not directly relevant for checking instance metadata. Instead, the correct API call should be `GET https://compute.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/{project}/zones/{zone}/instances/{instance}` to retrieve instance-specific metadata.
- The program name `gcp_compute_instance_os_login_enabled` is descriptive but could be more specific to indicate that it checks both project and instance-level settings.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- Correct the API call for instance metadata retrieval to `GET https://compute.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/{project}/zones/{zone}/instances/{instance}`.
- Enhance the program name to better reflect the dual-level check, such as `gcp_compute_os_login_project_instance_check`.
- Consider adding a check for the IAM roles associated with users to ensure they have the necessary permissions to use OS Login, which is a complementary aspect of managing SSH access through IAM.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured, and the use of GCP APIs and SDK commands is appropriate for the task. The minor inaccuracies identified do not significantly undermine the overall approach.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the API call for instance metadata retrieval and consider refining the program name for clarity.
